01_techniques: Describe the mechanisms of action of locoregional, neuraxial, acupuncture, and photobiomodulation techniques
Select and plan an appropriate locoregional block for a given surgical procedure and species
Apply correct laser fluence (J/cm²) parameters for a given indication and patient type
Identify absolute and relative contraindications to PBM, locoregional blocks, and acupuncture
Integrate multiple techniques into a synergistic perioperative or chronic pain management plan
Recognize and manage immediate complications of locoregional anesthesia (LAST, block failure)
Identify scope-of-competence boundaries and appropriate referral thresholds
02_neuro: Trace the nociceptive pathway from periphery to cortex, naming fiber types and neurotransmitters
Explain central sensitization, wind-up, and their clinical consequences for pain management
Describe descending inhibitory pathway anatomy and how analgesic drug classes engage it
Distinguish peripheral from central sensitization and their different treatment targets
Explain the neurophysiologic basis of allodynia and hyperalgesia
Identify species-specific neuroanatomic differences (avian, reptile) relevant to analgesia selection
Map major drug class mechanisms to specific receptor/pathway targets in the pain pathway
03_pharmacology: State the mechanism of action, key clinical properties, and major adverse effect for each analgesic drug class
Select and justify appropriate analgesics for patients with complicating conditions (renal disease, cardiac disease, species differences)
Identify high-risk drug combinations and explain the mechanism of harm
Build an opioid-sparing or opioid-free multimodal analgesic protocol for a specified patient
Apply species-specific pharmacologic constraints (feline NSAID limitations, butorphanol ceiling in cats)
Recognize opioid tolerance, dependence, and misuse in the clinical context
Describe controlled substance requirements relevant to opioid use in veterinary practice
04_non_pharm: Identify the evidence base and mechanism of analgesia for major non-pharmacological modalities
Build a species-specific non-pharmacological pain management plan for a companion animal
Integrate non-pharmacological modalities synergistically with pharmacological treatment
Recognize contraindications to specific non-pharmacological modalities
Counsel owners on environmental modifications and home-based pain management strategies
Apply weight management and nutritional principles in pain management planning
Identify when non-pharmacological modalities alone are insufficient and escalation is required
05_recognition_assessment: Distinguish validated acute pain scales from chronic pain clinical metrology instruments
Select and apply the appropriate validated pain assessment tool for a given species and pain context
Recognize subtle behavioral indicators of chronic pain in cats and exotic species
Apply the FAS scale and explain how fear, anxiety, and stress complicate pain assessment
Identify clinical signs distinguishing neuropathic from nociceptive pain
Classify surgical procedures by pain severity (high vs. low) and adjust analgesic planning accordingly
Operationalize pain as a vital sign in clinical workflow
Recognize common under-recognition pitfalls (prey species, geriatric patients, stoic breeds)
